response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist with strong full-stack and some systems-level background, but he falls well short of the core minimum qualifications. The role requires 8 years of C++ experience; his resume shows only about six months of professional C++ work (National Instruments) plus a thesis project. His infrastructure experience (low-latency DLang microservices at Sociomantic, Kubernetes deployment of a side project) is relevant but limited to roughly two years, and he has no formal technical-leadership or cloud-platform ownership experience of the kind this role demands.
strengths: High-throughput, low-latency systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering-practice culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot experience across backend, infrastructure tooling, and open-source project ownership (GISCollective)
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++ — only ~6 months of professional C++ work, a decade ago
No production-level cloud architecture or compute-platform ownership; infrastructure exposure is narrow and dated
No formal technical-lead role managing cross-team priorities, deadlines, and deliverables
